100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 8, 1926, the British government faced significant unrest as workers in the coal industry prepared for a national strike, raising concerns over escalating tensions between labor and management. Meanwhile, in the United States, the Senate was engaged in heated debates over the future of Prohibition, with calls for reform gaining momentum. In Europe, the aftermath of the recent Locarno Treaties continued to shape diplomatic relations, as nations sought stability in the wake of World War I.
